iterations.theme: Geopolitical Hotspots: Ukraine, Middle East, and European Diplomacy

sections.subtitle: Geopolitičko ljeto na rubu usijanja: Ukrajina i Bliski istok u fokusu
sections.screenshot_description: Marinko Gorec objašnjava zašto je pred nama 'vruće geopolitičko ljeto'.
sections.content: Uvod u podcast 'Mrežnica' započeo je pitanjem o nadolazećem 'vrućem geopolitičkom i vojnom ljetu'. Profesor Marinko Gorec, vojni analitičar, potvrdio je da će ljeto biti izuzetno dinamično, s neizvjesnim ishodom za jesen. Voditeljica je istaknula kako mira nema ni na Bliskom istoku, a ni između Ukrajine i Rusije. Gorec je iznio zanimljivu prognozu: vjeruje da bi se prije mogli dogovoriti bivši američki predsjednik Donald Trump i Iran, nego što bi se riješio sukob u Ukrajini. Smatra da je situacija s Iranom 'zrela za rješavanje' i da Trump traži izlaznu strategiju. S druge strane, u Ukrajini se dinamika borbenih djelovanja nastavlja, a trenutno nikome nije u interesu da se rat završi.
sections.subtitle_en: Geopolitical summer on the brink of overheating: Ukraine and the Middle East in focus
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marinko Gorec explains why a 'hot geopolitical summer' is ahead of us.
sections.content_en: The introduction to the podcast 'Mrežnica' began with a question about the upcoming 'hot geopolitical and military summer'. Professor Marinko Gorec, a military analyst, confirmed that the summer will be extremely dynamic, with an uncertain outcome for autumn. The host pointed out that there is no peace in the Middle East, nor between Ukraine and Russia. Gorec presented an interesting prognosis: he believes that former American president Donald Trump and Iran could reach an agreement sooner than the conflict in Ukraine would be resolved. He considers that the situation with Iran is 'ripe for resolution' and that Trump is looking for an exit strategy. On the other hand, in Ukraine, the dynamic of combat operations continues, and currently it is in no one's interest for the war to end.

sections.subtitle: Intenziviranje sukoba u Ukrajini: Ruski pritisak i ukrajinska obrana dronovima
sections.screenshot_description: Karta Ukrajine s označenim ključnim gradovima poput Kramatorska i Slavjanska.
sections.content: Profesor Gorec detaljno je opisao trenutnu situaciju na ukrajinskom bojištu, ističući kako su Rusi značajno pritisnuli Ukrajince na području posljednjih utvrda: Kramatorska, Slavjanska i Kupjanska. Jedan mali gradić, predstraža Kramatorska, pred padom je, a opskrbne linije su presječene. Unatoč tome, Ukrajinci se intenzivno brane, koncentrirajući snage na tom području. Zanimljivo je da Ukrajinci vrlo uspješno napadaju rusku logistiku duboko u ruskom teritoriju koristeći dronove, što usporava ruske operacije. Gorec je naglasio da su dronovi samo jedno od oružja, ali da budućnost donosi dronove vođene umjetnom inteligencijom i rojeve dronova koji autonomno djeluju na specifične ciljeve. Osvrnuo se i na ratnu propagandu, ističući da obje strane koriste laži kao sredstvo psihološkog djelovanja, što je razumljivo u ratnim uvjetima.
sections.subtitle_en: Intensification of the conflict in Ukraine: Russian pressure and Ukrainian defense with drones
sections.screenshot_description_en: Map of Ukraine with marked key cities such as Kramatorsk and Sloviansk.
sections.content_en: Professor Gorec described in detail the current situation on the Ukrainian battlefield, emphasizing how the Russians have significantly pressured the Ukrainians in the area of the last strongholds: Kramatorsk, Sloviansk, and Kupyansk. One small town, an outpost of Kramatorsk, is on the verge of falling, and supply lines have been cut. Despite this, the Ukrainians are intensely defending themselves, concentrating forces in that area. It is interesting that the Ukrainians are very successfully attacking Russian logistics deep within Russian territory using drones, which slows down Russian operations. Gorec emphasized that drones are only one of the weapons, but that the future brings AI-guided drones and drone swarms that autonomously act on specific targets. He also touched upon war propaganda, pointing out that both sides use lies as a means of psychological warfare, which is understandable in wartime conditions.

sections.subtitle: Misterij projektila 'Orešnik': Strateško oružje i legitimnost ciljeva
sections.screenshot_description: Grafički prikaz projektila 'Orešnik' i njegovih karakteristika.
sections.content: Rasprava se dotaknula i ruskog projektila 'Orešnik', za koji profesor Gorec smatra da je već bio korišten, vjerojatno na Beloj Crkvi, predgrađu Kijeva. Istaknuo je da se 'Orešnik' ne koristi za gađanje malih ciljeva, već je riječ o skupom i dragocjenom sustavu namijenjenom strateškim ili taktički važnim metama. Odbacio je sumnje u njegovu učinkovitost, naglašavajući da je riječ o balističkoj raketi srednjeg dometa koja nosi više autonomnih bojnih glava, od kojih svaka pogađa svoj cilj. Iako se trenutno koristi s kinetičkim punjenjem, ima potencijal nositi i nuklearno. Gorec je primijetio da nakon udara 'Orešnikom' obično zavlada 'tišina', što sugerira da su pogođeni ciljevi bili izuzetno važni za Ukrajince. U kontekstu međunarodnog prava, Gorec je objasnio složenost definiranja legitimnih ciljeva, posebno u asimetričnim sukobima. Postavio je pitanje krivnje kada se vojne mete skrivaju ispod civilnih objekata, poput bolnica, referirajući se na situaciju u Gazi. Slično je i s obavještajnim centrima trećih zemalja u Ukrajini – njihova legitimnost kao cilja ovisi o tome koliko ugrožavaju ruske interese.
sections.subtitle_en: Mystery of the 'Orešnik' missile: Strategic weapon and legitimacy of targets
sections.screenshot_description_en: Graphical representation of the 'Orešnik' missile and its characteristics.
sections.content_en: The discussion also touched upon the Russian missile 'Oreshnik', which Professor Gorec believes has already been used, probably in Bila Tserkva, a suburb of Kyiv. He pointed out that 'Oreshnik' is not used for targeting small objectives, but is a costly and valuable system intended for strategic or tactically important targets. He dismissed doubts about its effectiveness, emphasizing that it is a medium-range ballistic missile that carries multiple autonomous warheads, each of which hits its own target. Although it is currently used with a kinetic payload, it has the potential to carry a nuclear one as well. Gorec noted that after an 'Oreshnik' strike, 'silence' usually prevails, suggesting that the targets hit were extremely important to the Ukrainians. In the context of international law, Gorec explained the complexity of defining legitimate targets, especially in asymmetric conflicts. He raised the question of culpability when military targets are hidden beneath civilian objects, such as hospitals, referring to the situation in Gaza. It is similar with intelligence centers of third countries in Ukraine – their legitimacy as a target depends on how much they threaten Russian interests.

sections.subtitle: Ukrajinski domet i zapadna podrška: Bez SAD-a nema dubokih udara
sections.screenshot_description: Grafički prikaz dometa ukrajinskih projektila na ruskom teritoriju.
sections.content: Profesor Gorec procjenjuje da bi Ukrajinci mogli gađati ruski teritorij čak do Urala, ali uz ključnu pomoć Sjedinjenih Američkih Država. Naglasio je da za tako velike udaljenosti i preciznost Ukrajina treba sofisticirane sustave navođenja koje joj mogu pružiti samo SAD. Bez zapadne pomoći, posebno američke, ukrajinska ratna priča bi se vrlo brzo završila. Europska unija pruža značajnu financijsku pomoć, naoružanje i opremu, dok SAD doprinosi satelitskim snimkama, sustavima navođenja i obavještajnim podacima, što je od vitalnog značaja za ukrajinske operacije.
sections.subtitle_en: Ukrainian range and Western support: Without the USA there are no deep strikes.
sections.screenshot_description_en: Graphical representation of the range of Ukrainian missiles on Russian territory.
sections.content_en: Professor Gorec estimates that Ukrainians could target Russian territory even up to the Urals, but with crucial help from the United States of America. He emphasized that for such great distances and precision, Ukraine needs sophisticated guidance systems that only the USA can provide. Without Western aid, especially American, the Ukrainian war story would end very quickly. The European Union provides significant financial aid, weaponry, and equipment, while the USA contributes satellite imagery, guidance systems, and intelligence data, which is of vital importance for Ukrainian operations.

sections.subtitle: Pregovori i 'ratni jastrebovi': Europa na raskrižju s Rusijom
sections.screenshot_description: Prikaz europskih lidera i Putina, simbolizirajući pregovore.
sections.content: Profesor Gorec kritizirao je Europu zbog nedostatka pregovora s Rusijom, smatrajući da 'Europa sama sebi doslovno reže granu na kojoj sjedi'. Istaknuo je da su pregovori između Amerike i Rusije već započeli i da se nastavljaju, što je ključno za smirivanje situacije. Smatra da bi europski lideri, poput Ursule von der Leyen, trebali preuzeti inicijativu za dijalog s Putinom, a spomenuo je i bivše lidere poput Angele Merkel i Gerharda Schrödera kao osobe s kredibilitetom, iako bez trenutne političke pozicije. Gorec je odbacio ideju da Rusija želi napasti Europu, ističući da Rusiji Europa treba kao tržište, a ne kao devastirani prostor. Naglasio je da Rusija nema kapaciteta za napad na Europu s preko 500 milijuna stanovnika i snažnim oružanim snagama, te da 'nikada u povijesti slabiji nije napao jačega'. Također je istaknuo da Rusija neće ponoviti strateške pogreške iz Ukrajine. Osvrnuo se i na hibridno ratovanje i migracije kao destabilizirajuće faktore za Europu, te na stav Donalda Trumpa prema NATO-u, koji traži veća izdvajanja za obranu od europskih članica, što je nazvao 'cost-benefit' analizom.
sections.subtitle_en: Negotiations and 'war hawks': Europe at a crossroads with Russia
sections.screenshot_description_en: Depiction of European leaders and Putin, symbolizing negotiations.
sections.content_en: Professor Gorec criticized Europe due to the lack of negotiations with Russia, considering that 'Europe is literally cutting the branch it is sitting on'. He pointed out that negotiations between America and Russia have already begun and are continuing, which is crucial for calming the situation. He believes that European leaders, such as Ursula von der Leyen, should take the initiative for dialogue with Putin, and he also mentioned former leaders like Angela Merkel and Gerhard Schröder as credible individuals, albeit without current political positions. Gorec rejected the idea that Russia wants to attack Europe, emphasizing that Russia needs Europe as a market, not as a devastated area. He stressed that Russia does not have the capacity to attack Europe with over 500 million inhabitants and strong armed forces, and that 'never in history has the weaker attacked the stronger'. He also pointed out that Russia will not repeat the strategic mistakes from Ukraine. He also touched upon hybrid warfare and migrations as destabilizing factors for Europe, and on Donald Trump's stance towards NATO, which demands greater defense spending from European members, which he called a 'cost-benefit' analysis.

sections.subtitle: Ruske strateške pogreške i ukrajinska protuofenziva: Lekcije iz rata
sections.screenshot_description: Grafički prikaz ruskih i ukrajinskih vojnih operacija i linija sukoba.
sections.content: Profesor Gorec je najvećom ruskom pogreškom nazvao 'kriminalnu, katastrofalnu, potpuno pogrešnu procjenu situacije' prije invazije na Ukrajinu. Ta pogrešna procjena dovela je do ogromnih gubitaka u tehnici i ljudstvu te narušavanja kredibiliteta. S druge strane, Ukrajinci su u početku rata dočekali Ruse s neočekivanim otporom. Kasnije, kada su se Rusi konsolidirali, Ukrajinci su napravili pogrešku s neuspjelom velikom ofenzivom na 'Surovikinovu liniju' 2023. godine, gdje su se 'razbili'. Ipak, obje strane su se prilagodile, učeći iz grešaka i uvodeći nove oružane sustave. Gorec je istaknuo da su Rusi i Ukrajinci sličnog mentaliteta, s izraženom inovativnošću i sposobnošću improvizacije, što im pomaže u preživljavanju i ostvarivanju ciljeva u borbenim djelovanjima.
sections.subtitle_en: Russian strategic mistakes and Ukrainian counteroffensive: Lessons from the war
sections.screenshot_description_en: Graphical representation of Russian and Ukrainian military operations and lines of conflict.
sections.content_en: Professor Gorec called the biggest Russian mistake 'the criminal, catastrophic, completely wrong assessment of the situation' before the invasion of Ukraine. That wrong assessment led to huge losses in equipment and personnel and damage to credibility. On the other hand, the Ukrainians at the beginning of the war met the Russians with unexpected resistance. Later, when the Russians consolidated, the Ukrainians made a mistake with a failed large offensive on the 'Surovikin Line' in 2023, where they were 'broken'. Nevertheless, both sides adapted, learning from mistakes and introducing new weapon systems. Gorec pointed out that Russians and Ukrainians are of a similar mentality, with pronounced innovativeness and ability to improvise, which helps them in survival and achieving goals in combat operations.

sections.subtitle: Volinski masakr: Povijesne rane između Poljske i Ukrajine
sections.screenshot_description: Arhivska fotografija iz Drugog svjetskog rata koja prikazuje sukobe u Volinju.
sections.content: Profesor Gorec objasnio je nedavno poljsko nezadovoljstvo Ukrajinom, koje proizlazi iz povijesnih tenzija, posebno Volinskog masakra iz Drugog svjetskog rata. Nakon podjele Poljske između Njemačke i Sovjetskog Saveza, vojvodstvo Volinj pripalo je Sovjetima, a kasnije ga je preuzela ukrajinska vlast. Tijekom njemačke okupacije, dio ukrajinskog stanovništva, koje nije podnosilo Ruse, pokušao je ostvariti državotvornost uz podršku njemačkih snaga. To je dovelo do stvaranja Organizacije ukrajinskih nacionalista (OUN) pod vodstvom Stepana Bandere i Ukrajinske ustaničke vojske (UPA). Te su snage, uz sudjelovanje lokalnog ukrajinskog stanovništva, počinile masovne pokolje poljskog stanovništva u Volinju i Galiciji, s procjenama od 50.000 do preko 100.000 žrtava. Ovaj tragični događaj ostavio je duboke rane i ima reperkusije na današnje poljsko-ukrajinske odnose, čineći Poljake opreznima u odnosima s Ukrajinom.
sections.subtitle_en: Volyn massacre: Historical wounds between Poland and Ukraine
sections.screenshot_description_en: Archival photograph from the Second World War which depicts conflicts in Volhynia.
sections.content_en: Professor Gorec recently explained Polish dissatisfaction with Ukraine, which stems from historical tensions, especially the Volyn massacre from the Second World War. After the division of Poland between Germany and the Soviet Union, the Volyn Voivodeship belonged to the Soviets, and later Ukrainian authorities took it over. During the German occupation, a part of the Ukrainian population, which did not tolerate Russians, tried to achieve statehood with the support of German forces. This led to the creation of the Organization of Ukrainian Nationalists (OUN) under the leadership of Stepan Bandera and the Ukrainian Insurgent Army (UPA). These forces, with the participation of the local Ukrainian population, committed massacres of the Polish population in Volyn and Galicia, with estimates ranging from 50,000 to over 100,000 victims. This tragic event left deep wounds and has repercussions on today's Polish-Ukrainian relations, making Poles cautious in relations with Ukraine.

sections.subtitle: SAD i Iran: Pogrešne procjene, tajna diplomacija i izraelske ambicije
sections.screenshot_description: Grafički prikaz odnosa SAD-a, Irana i Izraela na karti Bliskog istoka.
sections.content: Profesor Gorec povukao je paralelu između ruskih pogrešnih procjena u Ukrajini i američkih u Iranu. Smatra da je bivši predsjednik Trump, pod utjecajem pobjede nad Madurom u Venezueli i sugestija Benjamina Netanyahua, pogrešno procijenio da će se situacija u Iranu ponoviti. Iako je Trump vjerojatno imao kvalitetne informacije od vojnih planera, on ih je ignorirao. Unatoč početnim neuspjesima, pregovori između SAD-a i Irana, uz posredovanje Pakistana, stalno traju, često kroz 'tajnu diplomaciju'. Gorec ističe da su prvi pregovori obično neuspješni zbog maksimalističkih očekivanja, ali da se pravi dogovori postižu iza kulisa. Naglasio je da je Izrael najveći gubitnik u tom procesu, jer mu je cilj biti dominantna regionalna sila na Bliskom istoku, a Iran doživljava kao glavnu prepreku. Izrael ne pristaje na prekid borbenih djelovanja, posebno u Libanonu. Zanimljivo je da Iran nije reagirao defenzivno na američko-izraelske napade, već je uzvratio udarima dalekometnim raketnim sustavima po Izraelu i američkim bazama, što je iznenadilo protivnike i dovelo do pregovaračkog stola. Ova iranska strategija 'napad je najbolja obrana' i sustav disperziranog vođenja, gdje niži zapovjednici imaju veliku autonomiju, predstavljaju nove lekcije za vojne škole.
sections.subtitle_en: USA and Iran: Miscalculations, secret diplomacy and Israeli ambitions
sections.screenshot_description_en: Graphical representation of the relations of the USA, Iran, and Israel on the map of the Middle East.
sections.content_en: Professor Gorec drew a parallel between Russian miscalculations in Ukraine and American ones in Iran. He believes that former President Trump, under the influence of the victory over Maduro in Venezuela and Benjamin Netanyahu's suggestions, miscalculated that the situation in Iran would repeat itself. Although Trump probably had quality information from military planners, he ignored them. Despite initial failures, negotiations between the US and Iran, with Pakistan's mediation, are constantly ongoing, often through 'secret diplomacy'. Gorec points out that the first negotiations are usually unsuccessful due to maximalist expectations, but that real agreements are reached behind the scenes. He emphasized that Israel is the biggest loser in that process, because its goal is to be the dominant regional power in the Middle East, and it perceives Iran as the main obstacle. Israel does not agree to a cessation of hostilities, especially in Lebanon. It is interesting that Iran did not react defensively to American-Israeli attacks, but retaliated with long-range missile system strikes on Israel and American bases, which surprised opponents and led to the negotiating table. This Iranian strategy 'attack is the best defense' and the system of dispersed command, where lower commanders have great autonomy, represent new lessons for military schools.

iterations.theme_en: Geopolitical Hotspots: Ukraine, Middle East, and European Diplomacy

iterations.theme: Military Evolution, Geopolitical Shifts, and the Nature of Power

sections.subtitle: Armenija: Geopolitički zaokret od Rusije
sections.screenshot_description: Profesor Gorec objašnjava armensko-ruske odnose i razočaranje Armenije.
sections.content: Voditeljica otvara temu geopolitičkog zaokreta Armenije, ističući njezinu važnost za Rusiju. Profesor Gorec objašnjava da je Armenija, kao dio nekadašnjeg Sovjetskog Saveza, imala poseban odnos s Rusijom. Međutim, taj se odnos mijenja, a Armenija, slično baltičkim zemljama, želi se osloboditi 'zagrljaja ruskog medvjeda'. Ključni pokretač ovog zaokreta bio je sukob između Azerbejdžana i Armenije, tijekom kojeg je Armenija osjetila da ju je Rusija izdala, jer nije pružila očekivanu pomoć. S druge strane, Azerbejdžan je imao stalnu podršku Turske. Rusija sada traži od Armenije da se jasno izjasni o svojoj budućoj orijentaciji, dok se u Armeniji očekuju izbori koji će odrediti hoće li zemlja krenuti pro-ruskim ili pro-europskim putem.
sections.screenshot_description_en: Professor Gorec explains Armenian-Russian relations and Armenia's disappointment.
sections.content_en: The host opens the topic of Armenia's geopolitical shift, emphasizing its importance for Russia. Professor Gorec explains that Armenia, as part of the former Soviet Union, had a special relationship with Russia. However, that relationship is changing, and Armenia, similar to the Baltic countries, wants to free itself from 'the embrace of the Russian bear'. The key driver of this shift was the conflict between Azerbaijan and Armenia, during which Armenia felt that Russia had betrayed it, because it did not provide the expected help. On the other hand, Azerbaijan had constant support from Turkey. Russia is now asking Armenia to clearly state its future orientation, while elections are expected in Armenia that will determine whether the country will take a pro-Russian or pro-European path.

sections.subtitle: Lipanj: Mjesec velikih vojnih kampanja i klimatski faktori
sections.screenshot_description: Profesor Gorec objašnjava utjecaj klime na vojne operacije.
sections.content: Voditeljica primjećuje da su se mnoge povijesne bitke, poput Dana D, Bitke kod Kurska, Waterlooa i Bitke za Midway, odvijale u lipnju. Profesor Gorec objašnjava da su klimatski uvjeti ključni faktor. Proljeće je često obilježeno otapanjem snijega, blatom i 'rasputicom' (teškim, blatnjavim terenom), što otežava vojne operacije. Lipanj, međutim, donosi stabilnije vrijeme, duže dane i suhe uvjete, idealne za borbena djelovanja. Ovi povoljni uvjeti nastavljaju se kroz srpanj, kolovoz i rujan, omogućujući intenzivne vojne aktivnosti koje su znatno teže provoditi u jesen, proljeće ili zimu zbog nepovoljnih terenskih uvjeta i ograničenja u korištenju tehnike.
sections.subtitle_en: June: Month of great military campaigns and climatic factors
sections.screenshot_description_en: Professor Gorec explains the influence of climate on military operations.
sections.content_en: The host notes that many historical battles, such as D-Day, the Battle of Kursk, Waterloo, and the Battle of Midway, took place in June. Professor Gorec explains that climatic conditions are a key factor. Spring is often characterized by melting snow, mud, and 'rasputica' (difficult, muddy terrain), which complicates military operations. June, however, brings more stable weather, longer days, and dry conditions, ideal for combat operations. These favorable conditions continue through July, August, and September, enabling intensive military activities that are significantly harder to conduct in autumn, spring, or winter due to unfavorable terrain conditions and limitations in the use of equipment.

sections.subtitle: Nove lekcije iz Ukrajine i Irana: Transformacija modernog ratovanja
sections.screenshot_description: Profesor Gorec objašnjava promjene u modernom ratovanju, uključujući 'zonu smrti' i disperzirano zapovijedanje.
sections.content:
Na pitanje o ključnim lekcijama iz ratova u Ukrajini i Iranu za vojne akademije, profesor Gorec ističe da se moderni sukobi značajno razlikuju od klasičnih operacija poput 'Oluje'.

**Ukrajina:** Tradicionalna bojišnica više ne postoji. Umjesto toga, razvila se 'zona smrti' ili 'zona isključenja' široka 20-30 kilometara, kojom dominiraju dronovi i besposadni sustavi. Ova zona je pod neprekidnim nadzorom brojnih senzora, od satelitskog izviđanja do malih dronova, koji detektiraju svaki pokret i odmah reagiraju borbenim sustavima. To čini konvencionalne operacije izuzetno teškima.

**Iran:** Sukob u Iranu (između američko-izraelske koalicije i Irana) također je pokazao odsustvo klasične bojišnice, s fokusom na dalekometne raketne sustave i zračne operacije. Dvije ključne lekcije iz Irana su:

*   **Neočekivani iranski odgovor:** Izraelsko-američka koalicija očekivala je klasičnu obranu Irana, s fokusom na protuzračnu obranu i mornaricu. Međutim, Iran je uzvratio izravnim napadima dalekometnim raketama na izraelske gradove i američke baze, što je iznenadilo obje strane i dovelo do pregovora. To je potvrdilo staru poslovicu da je napad najbolja obrana.
*   **Disperzirano zapovijedanje i vođenje:** Unatoč likvidaciji kompletnog iranskog vojnog i političkog vrha, sustav se nije raspao. Iranske oružane snage pokazale su izvanrednu autonomiju na nižim razinama zapovijedanja. Regionalni zapovjednici, bez kontakta s višim zapovjedništvom, samostalno su vodili operacije, koordinirajući sa susjedima kako bi održali tempo borbenih djelovanja. Ova fleksibilnost, neuobičajena za vojsku, osigurala je kontinuitet operacija i mogla bi postati model za buduće vojske.
sections.subtitle_en: New lessons from Ukraine and Iran: Transformation of modern warfare
sections.screenshot_description_en: Professor Gorec explains changes in modern warfare, including 'death zone' and dispersed command.
sections.content_en:
To the question about key lessons from the wars in Ukraine and Iran for military academies, Professor Gorec points out that modern conflicts significantly differ from classic operations like 'Storm'.

**Ukraine:** The traditional front line no longer exists. Instead, a 'death zone' or 'exclusion zone' 20-30 kilometers wide has developed, dominated by drones and unmanned systems. This zone is under continuous surveillance by numerous sensors, from satellite reconnaissance to small drones, which detect every movement and immediately react with combat systems. This makes conventional operations extremely difficult.

**Iran:** The conflict in Iran (between the American-Israeli coalition and Iran) also showed the absence of a classic front line, with a focus on long-range missile systems and air operations. Two key lessons from Iran are:

*   **Unexpected Iranian response:** The Israeli-American coalition expected a classic defense from Iran, with a focus on air defense and navy. However, Iran retaliated with direct attacks using long-range missiles on Israeli cities and American bases, which surprised both sides and led to negotiations. This confirmed the old proverb that attack is the best defense.
*   **Dispersed command and control:** Despite the liquidation of the entire Iranian military and political leadership, the system did not collapse. The Iranian armed forces showed extraordinary autonomy at lower levels of command. Regional commanders, without contact with higher command, independently led operations, coordinating with neighbors to maintain the tempo of combat operations. This flexibility, unusual for an army, ensured the continuity of operations and could become a model for future armies.

sections.subtitle: Hrvatsko iskustvo: Brzo stvaranje vojske u najgorim uvjetima
sections.screenshot_description: Profesor Gorec govori o stvaranju Hrvatske vojske i važnosti vojnog roka.
sections.content:
Voditeljica postavlja pitanje o vremenu potrebnom za stvaranje ozbiljne vojske. Profesor Gorec ističe hrvatsko iskustvo kao jedinstven primjer, gdje je vojska stvorena u iznimno kratkom roku i pod najgorim mogućim uvjetima. Hrvatska je, bez vlastitih oružanih snaga, u manje od godinu dana uspjela zaustaviti agresiju, oslanjajući se na policiju i motivirane građane koji su prošli vojni rok i znali koristiti oružje. Ključnu ulogu odigrali su i časnici iz bivše JNA te Legije stranaca, koji su svoje znanje i vještine ugradili u novonastale snage. Ipak, nedostatak resursa bio je veliki izazov.

Profesor Gorec kritizira razdoblje nakon 2000. godine, kada su Oružane snage Republike Hrvatske bile devastirane. Kao primjer navodi nedavni incident s ročnikom i 'gnojnom anginom', što smatra simptomom nesnalaženja zapovjednika i posljedicom ukidanja obveznog vojnog roka 2007. ili 2008. godine. Naglašava da je prekid vojnog roka bila katastrofalna pogreška koja je dovela do gubitka sposobnosti i iskustva, što se sada očituje u 'početničkim greškama' i medijskim farsama.
sections.subtitle_en: Croatian experience: Rapid creation of an army in the worst conditions
sections.screenshot_description_en: Professor Gorec speaks about the creation of the Croatian Army and the importance of military service.
sections.content_en: The host asks a question about the time needed for creating a serious army. Professor Gorec highlights the Croatian experience as a unique example, where the army was created in an exceptionally short period and under the worst possible conditions. Croatia, without its own armed forces, in less than a year managed to stop the aggression, relying on the police and motivated citizens who had completed military service and knew how to use weapons. A key role was also played by officers from the former JNA and the Foreign Legion, who incorporated their knowledge and skills into the newly formed forces. However, the lack of resources was a great challenge. Professor Gorec criticizes the period after 2000, when the Armed Forces of the Republic of Croatia were devastated. As an example, he cites a recent incident with a conscript and 'purulent tonsillitis', which he considers a symptom of commanders' incompetence and a consequence of the abolition of compulsory military service in 2007 or 2008. He emphasizes that the cessation of military service was a catastrophic mistake which led to a loss of capabilities and experience, which is now evident in 'beginner's mistakes' and media farces.

sections.subtitle: Putin, 'Siloviki' i ruski model vodstva: Snaga autoritarnosti
sections.screenshot_description: Profesor Gorec objašnjava ulogu 'Silovika' u ruskoj politici i Putinov uspon.
sections.content:
Voditeljica pita o evoluciji Vladimira Putina i je li on proizvod ruske politike ili zapadnih pogrešaka. Profesor Gorec objašnjava da je Putin proizvod problema s kojima se Rusija suočavala za vrijeme Borisa Jeljcina, kada je zemlja bila pred raspadom. Ta nestabilnost, posebno s obzirom na veliki nuklearni arsenal, bila je zabrinjavajuća i za Rusiju i za Zapad. Putina je na vlast postavila struktura koju Rusi nazivaju 'Siloviki' – državni aparat sastavljen od vojske, tajnih službi i obavještajnih agencija. Ova skupina tradicionalno bira i smjenjuje ruske vođe.

Gorec opisuje specifičan ruski model funkcioniranja države: Rusija napreduje pod čvrstim, beskompromisnim, sposobnim i autoritarnim vođama (poput Ivana Groznog, Staljina ili Putina), dok nazaduje pod slabim liderima (poput Nikolaja II. ili Jeljcina). Navodi anegdotu o starijim Rusima koji su Staljinovo doba smatrali vremenom stabilnosti i sigurnosti, unatoč neznanju o Gulazima i informativnoj blokadi. To pokazuje povijesnu sklonost Rusa prema snažnom vodstvu, čak i autoritarnom, u zamjenu za red i stabilnost. Profesor Gorec naglašava da 'Siloviki' i dalje podržavaju Putina, ali da će ga, kada dođe vrijeme, oni i zamijeniti, što može uključivati i njegovu likvidaciju, s obzirom na povijest 'čudnih smrti' ruskih lidera.
sections.subtitle_en: Putin, 'Siloviki' and the Russian model of leadership: The strength of authoritarianism
sections.screenshot_description_en: Professor Gorec explains the role of 'Siloviki' in Russian politics and Putin's rise.
sections.content_en: The host asks about the evolution of Vladimir Putin and whether he is a product of Russian politics or Western mistakes. Professor Gorec explains that Putin is a product of the problems Russia faced during Boris Jeljcin's time, when the country was on the verge of collapse. That instability, especially considering the large nuclear arsenal, was concerning for both Russia and the West. Putin was put in power by a structure that Russians call 'Siloviki' – a state apparatus composed of the military, secret services, and intelligence agencies. This group traditionally elects and removes Russian leaders. Gorec describes a specific Russian model of state functioning: Russia prospers under firm, uncompromising, capable, and authoritarian leaders (like Ivan Grozni, Staljin, or Putin), while it declines under weak leaders (like Nikolaj II or Jeljcin). He cites an anecdote about older Russians who considered the Stalin era a time of stability and security, despite their ignorance of the Gulags and the information blockade. This shows the historical inclination of Russians towards strong leadership, even authoritarian, in exchange for order and stability. Professor Gorec emphasizes that the 'Siloviki' still support Putin, but that when the time comes, they will also replace him, which may include his liquidation, given the history of 'strange deaths' of Russian leaders.

sections.subtitle: Transformacija ruske vojske: Od demoralizacije do modernizacije
sections.screenshot_description: Profesor Gorec opisuje drastične promjene u ruskoj vojsci pod Putinovim vodstvom.
sections.content:
Voditeljica uspoređuje rusku vojsku iz 1990-ih, koja je bila demoralizirana i podkapacitirana, s današnjom. Profesor Gorec ističe da je današnja ruska vojska 'potpuno drugačija', a za tu transformaciju zaslužni su prvenstveno Vladimir Putin i njegova administracija. Prisjeća se razdoblja kada je bio vojni ataše u Moskvi (2000.-2004.), kada je vojska bila u katastrofalnom stanju: zapovjednik nuklearne podmornice zarađivao je manje od vozača tramvaja, a visoki časnici radili su dodatne poslove kako bi prehranili obitelji. Mnogi mladi i sposobni ljudi napuštali su vojsku, što je dovelo do 'obrnute hijerarhije' s previše visokih časnika i premalo podređenih. Vojska je bila dodatno demoralizirana nakon poraza u Prvom čečenskom ratu.

Putin je, dolaskom na vlast, odmah poduzeo mjere: prva je bila deseterostruko povećanje vojnih plaća. Zatim je restrukturirao oružane snage, uklanjajući starije, neproduktivne časnike i obnavljajući vojno-industrijski kompleks. To je dovelo do proizvodnje i prodaje kvalitetnog naoružanja. Gruzijski rat 2008. bio je prvi test za reorganiziranu vojsku, koji su dobili u tri dana. Nakon toga uslijedile su nove reorganizacije, jedna poslije Gruzijskog rata i jedna prije ukrajinskog rata, a očekuje se još jedna nakon završetka trenutnog sukoba. Profesor Gorec zaključuje da današnja ruska vojska nije ni sjena one koju je zatekao početkom 2000-ih.
sections.subtitle_en: Transformation of the Russian army: From demoralization to modernization
sections.screenshot_description_en: Professor Gorec describes drastic changes in the Russian army under Putin's leadership.
sections.content_en: The host compares the Russian army from the 1990s, which was demoralized and under-capacitated, with today's. Professor Gorec points out that today's Russian army is 'completely different', and Vladimir Putin and his administration are primarily responsible for this transformation. He recalls the period when he was a military attaché in Moscow (2000-2004), when the army was in a catastrophic state: a nuclear submarine commander earned less than a tram driver, and high-ranking officers worked additional jobs to feed their families. Many young and capable people were leaving the army, which led to an 'inverted hierarchy' with too many high-ranking officers and too few subordinates. The army was further demoralized after the defeat in the First Chechen War. Upon coming to power, Putin immediately took measures: the first was a tenfold increase in military salaries. He then restructured the armed forces, removing older, unproductive officers and rebuilding the military-industrial complex. This led to the production and sale of quality weaponry. The 2008 Georgian War was the first test for the reorganized army, which they won in three days. After that, new reorganizations followed, one after the Georgian War and one before the Ukrainian War, and another is expected after the end of the current conflict. Professor Gorec concludes that today's Russian army is not even a shadow of the one he encountered in the early 2000s.

sections.subtitle: Globalne vojne sile: Kineska vojska i 'programirani' vojni duh
sections.screenshot_description: Profesor Gorec analizira kinesku vojnu paradu i vojni duh.
sections.content:
Voditeljica postavlja pitanje o najmoćnijim vojskama svijeta, s posebnim osvrtom na Kinu i njezino navodno neiskustvo u borbi. Profesor Gorec osporava ideju o Kini kao isključivo pacifističkoj sili, podsjećajući na okupaciju Tibeta kao dokaz njezinih ambicija. Naglašava da u međunarodnim odnosima postoji samo 'interes'. Savjetuje da se ne preuzimaju tuđe vojne recepture, već da svaka država gradi vlastite autonomne oružane snage, prilagođene svojim potrebama.

Profesor Gorec dijeli svoje dojmove s kineske vojne parade, ističući 'specifičnu odlučnost', 'izuzetno visoku organizaciju i hijerarhiju' te 'klonirani' izgled vojnika – iste visine, uniforme i načina razmišljanja. Posebno ističe 'isprogramiran' pogled kineskih vojnika, koji odražava usmjerenost na očekivane ishode. Zaključuje da Kina posjeduje vrlo ozbiljne oružane snage, ne samo u pogledu naoružanja i opreme, već i u mehanizmu izgradnje vojnog duha. Smatra da je kineska vojna struktura toliko moćna da im improvizacija, karakteristična za ukrajinske i ruske snage, možda uopće nije potrebna.
sections.subtitle_en: Global military forces: Chinese army and 'programmed' military spirit
sections.screenshot_description_en: Professor Gorec analyzes the Chinese military parade and the military spirit.
sections.content_en: The host asks a question about the most powerful armies in the world, with a special focus on China and its alleged inexperience in combat. Professor Gorec disputes the idea of China as an exclusively pacifist force, recalling the occupation of Tibet as proof of its ambitions. He emphasizes that in international relations there is only 'interest'. He advises not to adopt others' military recipes, but that each state builds its own autonomous armed forces, adapted to its needs. Professor Gorec shares his impressions from the Chinese military parade, highlighting 'specific determination', 'extremely high organization and hierarchy', and the 'cloned' appearance of soldiers – of the same height, uniform, and way of thinking. He particularly emphasizes the 'programmed' gaze of Chinese soldiers, which reflects a focus on expected outcomes. He concludes that China possesses very serious armed forces, not only in terms of weaponry and equipment, but also in the mechanism of building military spirit. He believes that the Chinese military structure is so powerful that improvisation, characteristic of Ukrainian and Russian forces, may not be needed at all.

sections.subtitle: Zaključak i 200. epizoda Podcasta Mrežnica
sections.screenshot_description: Voditeljica i profesor Gorec zaključuju podcast, obilježavajući 200. epizodu.
sections.content: Voditeljica zahvaljuje profesoru Gorecu na gostovanju u 200. epizodi 'Podcasta Mrežnica', a profesor Gorec izražava čast što je dio tog jubilarnog izdanja. Oboje ističu važnost povijesnog konteksta za razumijevanje aktualnih događaja i izražavaju nadu u buduću suradnju, čak i ako to znači nastavak razgovora o ratovima, s obzirom na trenutnu geopolitičku situaciju.
sections.subtitle_en: Conclusion and 200th episode of Mrežnica Podcast
sections.screenshot_description_en: The host and Professor Gorec conclude the podcast, marking the 200th episode.
sections.content_en: The host thanks Professor Gorec for guesting in the 200th episode of 'Podcast Mrežnica', and Professor Gorec expresses honor to be part of that jubilee edition. Both emphasize the importance of historical context for understanding current events and express hope for future cooperation, even if it means continuing the conversation about wars, given the current geopolitical situation.

iterations.theme_en: Military Evolution, Geopolitical Shifts, and the Nature of Power
